questions.text: When you face a hard problem, you naturally want to...

options.label: Collect data and analyse it systematically
Jump in and experiment hands-on until it works
Talk it through with people and get different views
Step back and imagine a completely new approach

questions.text: When the answer is unclear and you have to make a decision, you usually...

options.label: Look for rules or frameworks that apply to the situation
Trust your gut and act quickly
Research until you feel confident enough to decide
Ask someone with more experience and defer to them

questions.text: When you learn something new, you pick it up fastest by...

options.label: Reading and taking notes on your own
Watching someone do it and then copying them
Discussing it with others and asking questions
Just trying it out and figuring it out as you go

questions.text: Which of these achievements would make you feel most proud?

options.label: Publishing a discovery or finding that changes how people think
Building something that thousands of people use every day
Helping a person through a crisis and seeing them recover
Creating a piece of work that moves or inspires people

questions.text: What would frustrate you most in a work setting?

options.label: Doing the same routine task every day with no variety
Having to work alone with no one to talk to
Unclear goals — never knowing if my work is good enough
Being forced to follow rules I think are wrong or outdated

questions.text: When you have a deadline coming up, you most naturally...

options.label: Break it into a detailed schedule and follow it step by step
Push hard at the end — pressure makes me more focused
Start early so I have time to review and refine
Get others involved so we can split the work

questions.text: Which work setting sounds most appealing to you?

options.label: A hospital ward or clinic — busy, full of patients and colleagues
A quiet research lab — focused, methodical, mostly solo
A modern office or remote desk — structured, predictable, digital
Outdoors — forests, fields, construction sites, or the sea

questions.text: Which type of workspace would feel most like yours?

options.label: A creative studio full of screens, sketchbooks, and music
A courtroom, government office, or formal boardroom
A workshop or factory floor with machines and tools
A stage, studio, or performance venue

questions.text: Which sounds most like your ideal typical workday?

options.label: Meeting lots of different people and solving their problems
Deep focus on a single complex task for hours at a time
Managing a team, making decisions, and directing projects
Moving around — travelling, visiting sites, meeting different groups

questions.text: How important is it to you that your workspace is physically comfortable and predictable?

options.label: Very — I work best in a calm, organised, controlled setting
Not important — I thrive in dynamic, sometimes unpredictable environments
Somewhat — I like variety but with some structure
I prefer the outdoors or working with my hands regardless of comfort

questions.text: Would you be okay with irregular hours — night shifts, on-call duties, or unpredictable schedules?

options.label: Yes, that kind of urgency gives me energy
I can handle it short-term but I want predictable hours long-term
No — I want clear work-life separation
I want flexibility to set my own schedule, not someone else's shifts

questions.text: Does the idea of working in another city or even another country appeal to you?

options.label: Absolutely — I want an international career
I am open to it if the opportunity is good
I prefer to stay in my home city — roots matter to me
I want to work across India — rural and urban both

questions.text: How much do you want to interact with other people as part of your daily work?

options.label: A lot — I feel most alive when talking, teaching, or helping others
Some — I like working with a small trusted team
Minimal — I prefer working with data, tools, or systems
It depends on the day — I want a mix

questions.text: Would you prefer a job where you are the face people see, or one where you work behind the scenes?

options.label: Definitely front-facing — I like being the person others come to
Behind the scenes — I want my work to speak, not me
I want to influence decisions without being in the spotlight
I want to build a brand and be publicly known in my field

questions.text: Does the idea of teaching or training others appeal to you?

options.label: Yes — explaining things and watching people understand is deeply satisfying
Sometimes — I can mentor but I don't want it to be my whole job
Not really — I'd rather be the expert doing the work myself
No — I prefer creating things over explaining them

questions.text: How comfortable are you with helping people who are emotionally distressed or in crisis?

options.label: Very comfortable — I stay calm and feel called to help
Comfortable in structured situations, like a hospital or counselling room
I prefer helping people in practical, non-emotional ways
I find it draining — I prefer working with systems or ideas

questions.text: Imagine you are working in a team. Which role do you naturally drift towards?

options.label: The one who does deep research and brings the facts
The one who keeps the team aligned and motivated
The one who generates the ideas and sparks creativity
The one who gets things done reliably without drama

questions.text: Would you want clients or patients to know you personally and build a long relationship with you?

options.label: Yes — ongoing relationships are the most meaningful part of work
Somewhat — I like connecting but I don't want it to consume my work
No — I prefer each interaction to be task-specific and brief
I want to reach many people at scale, not just one at a time

questions.text: How do you feel about work where a small mistake could seriously harm someone?

options.label: I welcome that responsibility — it keeps me sharp and focused
I can handle it in certain conditions — like surgery or air traffic control
I'd prefer work where mistakes are recoverable and I can learn from them
I'd rather work in a low-stakes, steady environment

questions.text: Which type of pressure sounds most motivating to you?

options.label: Competition — rankings, exams, performance metrics
Creative deadlines — a film, an exhibition, a product launch
Humanitarian urgency — lives or communities at stake
Business pressure — revenue, growth, market share

questions.text: Would you be willing to spend 6–8 years in intense education before earning well, if the payoff is significant?

options.label: Yes, absolutely — the long game is worth it
Only if the career is one of my top passions
I prefer a faster route to earning and growing
I want to earn while I learn — through internships or skill-building

questions.text: Building something from scratch — your own business or practice — is something you...

options.label: Dream about — I want to create something that is truly mine
Would consider once I have enough experience and savings
Am not interested in — I want to excel in an established field
Find too risky — I value job security over ownership

questions.text: How do you feel about government or public sector jobs — secure, pensioned, service-oriented?

options.label: Very appealing — stability and public service are important to me
Good as a foundation — but I want to move to private sector eventually
Not for me — I want faster growth and higher income
Maybe for the impact on society, not for stability or pay

questions.text: Which part of medicine excites you most?

options.label: Performing surgeries and procedures with my hands
Diagnosing complex cases and figuring out what is wrong
Understanding the mind — mental illness, behaviour, emotions
Improving health systems and preventing disease across populations

questions.text: Emergency situations — a patient coding, a trauma case — make you feel...

options.label: Focused and energised — I was built for this
Capable but relieved when it is over — I prefer planned work
I would rather prevent emergencies than respond to them
Very anxious — I do not want high-pressure emergency work

questions.text: Long-term patient relationships — knowing your patients over years and following their journey — sounds...

options.label: Wonderful — that continuity of care is why I want to be in medicine
Nice but I'd prefer to specialise and see cases rather than people
I prefer acute interactions — intense but time-limited
Not for me — I want to work in research or administration

questions.text: Which healthcare role feels most like you?

options.label: A nurse — hands-on care, direct patient contact, day-to-day impact
A specialist doctor — deep expertise in one system or organ
A psychiatrist or counsellor — the emotional and mental side of health
A hospital administrator or health policy expert

questions.text: Reading medical research papers and running clinical trials sounds...

options.label: Fascinating — I want to contribute to medical knowledge
Useful but I want to apply findings, not generate them
Boring — I want to be with patients, not papers
Interesting from a policy angle — how findings become guidelines

questions.text: Which would bother you more over a long career?

options.label: Seeing too much death and suffering — emotional burnout
Being stuck in an office doing paperwork and administration
Knowing I am never cutting-edge — always using the same old methods
Not being recognised or respected by patients and colleagues

questions.text: The dentistry and physiotherapy path — independent practice, technical skill, direct patient care — sounds...

options.label: Very appealing — precise, hands-on, and I can build my own clinic
Decent — but I want broader impact than one patient at a time
Not for me — I want something more intellectually complex
I have not thought about it — health careers feel too intense for me

questions.text: What sounds most exciting about technology to you?

options.label: Writing code and building apps that millions of people use
Analysing huge datasets to find patterns no one has seen before
Designing how apps and websites look and feel to users
The hardware side — chips, circuits, servers, physical systems

questions.text: In a tech company, which team would you most want to be on?

options.label: Product — deciding what gets built and why
Backend engineering — the systems that power everything
Data science or AI — making the product smarter
Security — protecting systems from attacks

questions.text: Does the idea of physical engineering — building bridges, designing engines, planning cities — appeal to you?

options.label: Yes — I want to build things you can touch and see in the real world
Somewhat — but I'm more interested in the software side
Only if it involves cutting-edge technology, like aerospace or robotics
No — I prefer digital and abstract systems

questions.text: How do you feel about cybersecurity and network infrastructure?

options.label: Genuinely exciting — protecting systems is like a puzzle and a mission
Important but not my focus — I want to build things, not protect them
I would like it at a policy level — digital governance and law
I have not thought much about it — tech infrastructure sounds dry to me

questions.text: Which best describes your relationship with maths and logic?

options.label: I love it — the more abstract and complex, the better
I am good at it and use it practically, but it is not a passion
I prefer logic in the form of design or storytelling
Maths is not my strong suit — I prefer words, people, or physical work

questions.text: Artificial intelligence and machine learning — which role interests you most?

options.label: Building and training the models
Applying AI to solve real-world problems in a specific domain
The ethics and policy side — who controls AI and how
I am more interested in other fields — AI does not excite me particularly

questions.text: If you were building a startup, what kind would it be?

options.label: A software product or app
A hardware or deep-tech company
A design or creative agency
I would not start a startup — I want to work in an established organisation

questions.text: Which part of the business world interests you most?

options.label: Starting and growing a company — the whole entrepreneurial journey
Analysing companies and markets — investing and valuing assets
Auditing, accounting, and making sure the numbers are right
Marketing — understanding what people want and persuading them

questions.text: The idea of working in a bank or financial institution sounds...

options.label: Ideal — structured, prestigious, and high-earning
Interesting if it is trading or investment analysis
Too rigid — I want something with more creativity or purpose
I would consider fintech — combining finance with technology

questions.text: Management consulting — flying to different cities, solving business problems for large companies — sounds...

options.label: Exciting — variety, high impact, smart colleagues
Appealing but I would rather build one thing than advise many
Too corporate and shallow — I want work with deeper meaning
Too much travel and instability for my taste

questions.text: How do you feel about following strict financial regulations and compliance rules?

options.label: Fine with it — rules create systems I can master and work within
I respect the rules but want to find creative solutions within them
I prefer roles where I set the direction rather than follow compliance
Rules matter but I want the role where I design the regulations

questions.text: Economics as a field — understanding how economies work at a country or global scale — interests you...

options.label: A lot — it is one of the most powerful lenses for understanding the world
In a practical applied way — I want to use it, not study it academically
As part of a broader social science interest
Not much — I prefer more concrete fields

questions.text: If you had ten lakh rupees to use, you would most likely...

options.label: Invest it across stocks, mutual funds, and bonds
Start a small business or fund a side project
Fund my education or skill development
Use it for a creative project or experience

questions.text: Which creative format excites you most?

options.label: Visual — illustration, graphic design, photography
Written — stories, journalism, essays, scripts
Performance — acting, music, dance, standup
Film and video — direction, cinematography, editing

questions.text: In creative work, do you prefer creating something alone or collaborating with a team?

options.label: Alone — I have a clear vision and I want to execute it myself
With a team — great creative work comes from diverse inputs
I direct a team — my vision, their skills
I prefer co-creation — building something together with no single owner

questions.text: Does teaching or educating others feel like a calling to you?

options.label: Yes — few things are as meaningful as shaping how someone thinks
Yes, but at a higher level — university, research, or policy
Somewhat — I might teach, but I want creative freedom too
Not particularly — I'd rather create than teach

questions.text: Journalism — investigating stories, interviewing people, writing under deadline — sounds...

options.label: Exciting — I want to be the person who uncovers and tells the truth
Interesting but I'd focus on analysis — data journalism or long-form
Too stressful — I prefer creative work with fewer hard deadlines
Not my path — I'm more interested in doing things than reporting them

questions.text: The music industry — performing, producing, composing — is something you...

options.label: Are deeply passionate about and could see as a career
Love as a hobby but would not pursue professionally
Could see from the technical or production side — audio engineering
Have little interest in

questions.text: A graphic designer vs a UX designer vs an architect — which appeals most?

options.label: Graphic designer — visual aesthetics, brand identity, and illustration
UX designer — how digital products feel to use, human-centred design
Architect — blending art, engineering, and space to build the physical world
None of these — I prefer a different path

questions.text: Pure research — spending years on a single question, writing papers, working in labs — sounds...

options.label: Deeply fulfilling — I want to be at the frontier of human knowledge
Interesting but I want faster real-world impact
Too solitary and slow for me
I like the research but want to apply it in industry, not academia

questions.text: The law — courtrooms, contracts, rights, and justice — interests you because...

options.label: I find arguing and debating deeply satisfying
I believe in a just system and want to work within it
Corporate law and business transactions interest me more
It does not particularly interest me

questions.text: The IAS or IPS — leading districts, managing crises, shaping policy — sounds...

options.label: Like one of the most meaningful careers an Indian can have
Appealing for the power and respect, but the preparation is too intense
Not for me — I want private sector freedom and earnings
I would prefer policy without the IAS — think tanks, NGOs, or international orgs

questions.text: Environmental science and conservation — protecting ecosystems, studying climate, working in the field — sounds...

options.label: Exactly the kind of work I find meaningful
Important but I would address it from a policy or tech angle
Interesting but too low-paying for my goals
Not a priority for me in career terms

questions.text: Social work and welfare — working in communities, NGOs, or with marginalised groups — sounds...

options.label: Deeply fulfilling — this is the kind of impact I want
Good but I want systemic change — policy rather than direct service
Admirable but not for me — I need financial security
I want to do this alongside another career — not as my main job

questions.text: Diplomacy and international relations — representing India abroad, negotiating treaties — sounds...

options.label: Exciting — I love the idea of combining language, culture, and strategy
Interesting but I would prefer international business over government service
Worth considering if I join through the IFS
Not appealing — too political and slow-moving

questions.text: Which matters most to you in a career, right now?

options.label: High income — I want to earn well and build financial security fast
Meaning and social impact — I want my work to matter
Mastery — becoming the best in the world at one specific thing
Freedom and autonomy — control over my time and how I work

questions.text: Job stability versus opportunity — which side do you lean?

options.label: Stability — I want a secure career with guaranteed income
Opportunity — I am willing to take risks for bigger rewards
A balance — stable foundation in early career, then take calculated risks
Growth and learning matter more than stability or risk right now

questions.text: Deep specialist versus broad generalist — which describes your ambition?

options.label: Specialist — I want to be the go-to expert in one narrow area
Generalist — I want to connect ideas across many fields
Deep in one area, but with a broad strategic view
I have not decided yet — I am still exploring

questions.text: How much does public recognition matter to you?

options.label: A lot — I want to be known and respected in my field
Somewhat — recognition within my industry or community
Not much — I want personal satisfaction over fame
I want to be known for results, not personality

questions.text: City life versus rural or remote work — where do you see yourself?

options.label: Major Indian city or abroad — I want urban energy and opportunity
Smaller city — good quality of life with a real career
I would happily work in rural or remote areas for the right mission
Wherever the work takes me — location is not a priority

questions.text: Which would bother you more after 10 years in a career?

options.label: Not having earned enough to be financially comfortable
Feeling like my work made no real difference to anyone
Never reaching mastery — still feeling like a beginner
Feeling trapped — no creativity, no autonomy, no growth

questions.text: How important is it that your work contributes to something bigger than yourself — a cause, a mission, or a movement?

options.label: Essential — I cannot imagine a career without a larger purpose
Important but I also need the work to be personally fulfilling
Nice to have, but not a deciding factor
I prefer to define my own mission — not follow someone else's cause

questions.text: How do you feel about continuous learning — updating your skills and knowledge throughout your career?

options.label: I thrive on it — I want a career where the learning never stops
I am willing to do it but I want deep mastery first
I prefer once I master something, to keep doing it excellently
I want to learn broadly — many fields, not just one

questions.text: Which of these future versions of yourself sounds most right?

options.label: A respected expert who is the first call when the problem is hard
A leader who has built something — a company, institution, or movement
A person known for their creative work — writing, art, performance, or design
Someone quietly doing great work and living a fulfilling, balanced life

questions.text: Your parents and family's expectations for your career — how much do they influence you?

options.label: A lot — I want to make them proud and follow a respected path
Somewhat — I respect their input but the final call is mine
Minimally — I want to follow what genuinely excites me
It depends — I want to balance my passion with a practical career
